judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit every expectation with equivalent, correctly grounded findings; they are comparable, with Report 1 marginally more detailed on the OIDC role-collision reasoning.

report_1.evidence: Line 2 `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"` — developer debug tooling must not run in prod. Remove the key entirely ... Line 3 `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"` ... Remove the key entirely ... Line 4 `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"` — a test account with superadmin privileges. Remove entirely ... `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = ...` ... Remove both keys entirely. ... Line 7 `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = ...` — a legacy secret reference carried over from staging ... Remove

report_2.evidence: All in `medislot/infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars` ... **Line 2, `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`** ... **Line 3, `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`** ... **Line 4, `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`** ... The sink must be set to `""` ... **Line 7, `LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN = ...`**

report_1.evidence: the deploy-prod job (deploy.yml:29-35) deploys **the same already-built digest** to prod. So the production frontend would ship with the staging API URL baked in, with no runtime override possible. There is no `frontend/.env.production` in the repo and no prod build step; both are needed (rebuild per environment rather than promoting the digest

report_2.evidence: That **same digest** is deployed to both staging and prod ... Because the build runs with `--mode staging`, Vite loads `frontend/.env.staging`, which sets `VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com`. ... the production deployment will ship a bundle hard-wired to the staging API.** Either a `frontend/.env.production` plus a separate production build is needed

report_1.evidence: **No migration step**: `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in `deploy.yml` (nor any script...) runs the three migrations in `backend/migrations/versions/` ... on the first prod deploy the schema (`0001_base`, `0002_tenants`, `0003_billing`) would need a manual first pass.

report_2.evidence: the runbook ... claims migrations are applied automatically during deploy, but ... nothing in the workflow runs the three migrations ... For a first launch, the production database needs all three applied from scratch, and nothing verifiable does that.

report_1.evidence: For production, `infra/prod/main.tf:3` passes the **identical staging condition**, so the prod deploy role would (a) trust the staging environment's tokens instead of the `deploy-prod` job's `environment: production` tokens ... Prod needs its own role name and its own `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production` trust condition, never one shared with the looser environment.

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf` line 3 was copied unedited and still says `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"`. So the production deploy role trusts only staging-environment tokens. ... (b) conversely, staging-environment jobs *would* be trusted by the production role — an environment-separation violation.
